summary: R2 fixes the systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES blocker in sim.record-muster-response. The command now enforces disjoint authority modes: debtor-authorized responses may advance assembledTroops, delayedTroops, or refusedTroops without increasing releasedTroops; creditor-authorized releases may advance releasedTroops only while assembled/delayed/refused remain unchanged. Mixed response-plus-release advancement is rejected before authority selection, preventing the creditor/campaign owner from fabricating debtor assembly while releasing.

tests_run.result: Passed: 16 files, 113 tests. New R2 regression covers creditor mixed release/assembly fabrication rejection, debtor release authority denial, creditor mixed release/delayed/refused rejection, creditor over-release rejection, and legitimate debtor-response-then-creditor-release acceptance.
Passed: 1 file, 8 tests. Command schema shape is unchanged in R2.
Node and worker-compatible deterministic hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Prettier format check, ESLint, workspace typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.
Architecture boundaries passed, determinism passed, root Vitest passed 27 files / 180 tests, and web package tests passed 1 file / 3 tests.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-MUSTER-PREP-COMMITMENTS-001
tests_run.result: Parsed task JSON and verified expected task id, required scope/test arrays, status IN_PROGRESS, and risk R3.

decisions: Kept the existing sim.record-muster-response schema for the smallest focused fix; no protocol split was needed.
Added monotonic mode detection inside validateMusterResponsePayload: debtor response advancement and creditor release advancement cannot occur in the same command.
Kept release authority determined only after validation proves release is the sole advancing quantity, so a creditor cannot bundle debtor-owned assembled/delayed/refused changes with release.
Preserved player/AI parity because the same GameCommand path and actor authority checks apply to both actor kinds.
risks: R3 remains in effect because the task changes authoritative command/state behavior and protocol surface.
Save-format persistence for M4 muster state remains intentionally out of scope for this task and is unchanged by R2.
requested_action: Review the R2 response-authority fix for M4-MUSTER-PREP-COMMITMENTS-001 and route to systems_architect thread 019f0152-54c5-7d40-a504-dfc1a75082e1. Do not mark ACCEPTED or CLOSED until reviewer acceptance.
